title: Hjuksevelta

infobox.value: This [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") location article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Hjuksevelta&action=edit).

or simply **Hjukse** is a village in [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). The village is located about half-way between the [town of Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)") and the village of [Nordagutu](/wiki/Nordagutu "Nordagutu") (in [Midt-Telemark Municipality](/wiki/Midt-Telemark_(municipality) "Midt-Telemark (municipality)"). The village is located on the hills overlooking the eastern shore of the lake [Heddalsvatnet](/wiki/Heddalsvatnet "Heddalsvatnet"). In 2020, the Hjuksebø area became part of Notodden Municipality. Prior to that time, it was part of the old [Sauherad Municipality](/wiki/Sauherad_Municipality "Sauherad Municipality").[[3]](#cite_note-snl-3)

**Hjuksevelta** or simply **Hjukse** is a village in [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). The village is located about half-way between the [town of Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)") and the village of [Nordagutu](/wiki/Nordagutu "Nordagutu") (in [Midt-Telemark Municipality](/wiki/Midt-Telemark_(municipality) "Midt-Telemark (municipality)"). The village is located on the hills overlooking the eastern shore of the lake [Heddalsvatnet](/wiki/Heddalsvatnet "Heddalsvatnet"). In 2020, the Hjuksebø area became part of Notodden Municipality. Prior to that time, it was part of the old [Sauherad Municipality](/wiki/Sauherad_Municipality "Sauherad Municipality").[[3]](#cite_note-snl-3)

The 0.35-square-kilometre (86-acre) village has a population (2022) of 271 and a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 765 inhabitants per square kilometre (1,980/sq mi).[[1]](#cite_note-ssb-1)

The village of [Hjuksebø](/wiki/Hjukseb%C3%B8 "Hjuksebø") and the old [Hjuksebø Station](/wiki/Hjukseb%C3%B8_Station "Hjuksebø Station") are located just south of this village.[[3]](#cite_note-snl-3)
